content.text: Answer FOUR questions in detail. State carefully any results used without proof.

body.text:
Let \((a_n)\) be a bounded real sequence. Let
\[
U=\{u:(\exists N)(\forall n\geq N)(a_n<u)\}
\]
and
\[
L=\{\ell:(\exists N)(\forall n\geq N)(a_n>\ell)\}.
\]
Prove that \(\sup L\) and \(\inf U\) exist and satisfy \(\sup L\leq\inf U\).

Let \(X\) be the set of all bounded real-valued functions on the set \(S\). Prove that the rule
\[
d(f,g)=\sup\{|f(s)-g(s)|:s\in S\}
\]
defines a metric on \(X\). Are closed and bounded subsets of \(X\) compact when \(S\) is finite? Explain.

subparts.label: (i)
subparts.text: Prove that if \((x_n)_{n=0}^{\infty}\) converges to \(x\) in a metric space then the set \(\{x_n:n\in\mathbb{N}\}\cup\{x\}\) is compact.

subparts.label: (ii)
subparts.text: Prove that the function \(f:X\to Y\) between metric spaces is continuous if and only if its restriction \(f|_K\) to each compact subset \(K\) of \(X\) is continuous.

body.text: Let \(f:X\to Y\) be a map between metric spaces. Consider the statements: Prove or disprove each of the implications (i) \(\Rightarrow\) (ii) and (ii) \(\Rightarrow\) (i).

subparts.label: (i)
subparts.text: \(f\) maps Cauchy sequences to Cauchy sequences;

subparts.label: (i)
subparts.text: For \(h>0\) fixed, calculate the limit of \((f(x+h)-f(x))/x\) as \(x\to\infty\).

subparts.label: (ii)
subparts.text: Determine \(b\) if it is also known that \(f(x)\to a\in\mathbb{R}\) as \(x\to\infty\).
